result.admin_agent: ALTER DOMUS (US) LLC, as Administrative Agent
result.collateral_agent: ALTER DOMUS (US) LLC, as Collateral Agent
result.guarantors: Each Guarantor party to the Agreement (collectively with each Borrower, the "Loan Parties"); Foreign Guarantors under the Amended and Restated Foreign Guaranty; Luxembourg Guarantors (a Guarantor incorporated in Luxembourg). Note: "no Loan Party (other than Parent) shall form or acquire any Subsidiary after the Closing Date."
ebitda_addback_categories: Consolidated EBITDA add-backs as defined in the Trinseo Credit Agreement, with clause (x) add-backs capped at 10% of pro forma Consolidated EBITDA (after giving effect to such add-back and all other permitted add-backs) for any period; Aristech and Altuglas treated as 'Restricted Subsidiaries' for purposes of calculating Consolidated EBITDA

financial_covenants.kind: First Lien Net Leverage Ratio — springing mandatory prepayment trigger (post-JV Sale)
financial_covenants.test_frequency: Tested on a pro forma basis as of the most recently ended Test Period following the JV Sale
financial_covenants.springing_trigger: Following the JV Sale, if First Lien Net Leverage Ratio exceeds 3.75:1.00 as of the most recently ended Test Period AND the Lead Borrower receives any Excess Interest or Excess Amortization
financial_covenants.quote: Following the JV Sale, if (A) the First Lien Net Leverage Ratio, calculated on a pro forma basis in accordance with Section 1.10 of the Trinseo Credit Agreement, exceeds 3.503.75:1.00 as of the most recently ended Test Period ... following the JV Sale and (B) the Lead Borrower receives any Excess Interest or Excess Amortization at any time following the JV Sale, the Borrowers shall cause to be prepaid ... an aggregate principal amount of Term Loans in an amount equal to 100% of Excess Interest and Excess Amortization

baskets.name: Americas Styrenics Irregular Dividend prepayment threshold (Annual Threshold)
baskets.quote: Upon the receipt by the Co-Borrower of any cash dividends from Americas Styrenics ... in excess of $5,000,000 in any fiscal year (the "Annual Threshold"), the Co-Borrower shall cause to be prepaid ... 100% of such Irregular Dividends in excess of the Annual Threshold

baskets.name: Debt incurrence mandatory prepayment sweep
baskets.value: 100% of proceeds of Indebtedness (other than Indebtedness not prohibited under Section 7.03)
baskets.quote: If any Loan Party incurs or issues any Indebtedness (other than Indebtedness not prohibited under Section 7.03), the Borrowers shall cause to be prepaid an aggregate principal amount of Term Loans in an amount equal to

baskets.name: Restricted Payments (Section 7.06) / Restricted Payment definition
baskets.value: Not quantified in provided text; Restricted Payment defined to include dividends/distributions and Restricted Investments
baskets.quote: (i) any dividend or other distribution (whether in cash, securities or other property) with respect to any Equity Interest of any Loan Party ... and (ii) any Restricted Investment.

baskets.name: Permitted Investments (existing ownership + intercompany + JV + cash equivalents + Term Loan investments)
baskets.value: Enumerated categories only; no general dollar basket in provided text
baskets.quote: Investments consisting of the 2023 Incremental Term Loans and, the 2023 Refinancing Term Loans, the 2025 Incremental Term Loans or any proceeds or distributions therefrom

result.unrestricted_subsidiary_designation_flex: The text does not reproduce a general unrestricted-subsidiary designation covenant; however, it documents that Aristech and Altuglas were each designated as "Unrestricted Subsidiaries" under the Trinseo Credit Agreement, the 2029 Senior Notes and the Super-Priority Revolving Credit Agreement as a condition to the Third Amendment, with corresponding lien/guarantee releases and release of the Specified IP. This confirms the underlying documents permit unrestricted-subsidiary designation and asset/IP release, though for Consolidated EBITDA calculation Aristech and Altuglas are still treated as Restricted Subsidiaries.
result.anchor_clause: (e) ... (i) evidence satisfactory to the Required Lenders that each of Aristech and Altuglas have each been designated by the Board of Directors of the Trinseo Lead Borrower as an "Unrestricted Subsidiary" ... in accordance with and pursuant to the Trinseo Credit Agreement, the 2029 Senior Notes and the Super-Priority Revolving Credit Agreement ... and (iii) intellectual property security interest releases with respect to the Specified IP under the Trinseo Credit Agreement

lme_vectors.rationale: This very amendment effectuates a dropdown-style transaction: Aristech and Altuglas were designated Unrestricted Subsidiaries and the Specified IP was released from the collateral package, demonstrating live capacity to move material IP/assets outside the credit group.
lme_vectors.basis: Section 7.14 (Specified IP Covenant) exists, but conditions precedent (e) confirm Aristech/Altuglas were designated Unrestricted Subsidiaries and Specified IP liens were released as part of the deal.
lme_vectors.quote: intellectual property security interest releases with respect to the Specified IP under the Trinseo Credit Agreement, in each case in form and substance satisfactory to the Administrative Agent and the Required Lenders

lme_vectors.rationale: The agreement references Incremental Credit Extensions (Section 2.16) and 2025 Incremental Term Loans, and permits Indebtedness not prohibited under Section 7.03, but Section 7.03 text and any incremental cap/MFN are not reproduced, so capacity cannot be sized.
lme_vectors.basis: Section 7.03 (Indebtedness); Section 2.16 (Incremental Credit Extensions) referenced; mandatory prepayment sweep excludes 'Indebtedness not prohibited under Section 7.03'.
lme_vectors.quote: If any Loan Party incurs or issues any Indebtedness (other than Indebtedness not prohibited under Section 7.03), the Borrowers shall cause to be prepaid an aggregate principal amount of Term Loans

lme_vectors.rationale: Section 7.06 governs Restricted Payments and the RP definition is provided, but the actual permitted-RP baskets and dollar thresholds are not reproduced in the excerpt, so leakage capacity cannot be assessed.
lme_vectors.basis: Section 7.06 (Restricted Payments); definition of Restricted Payment covering dividends, distributions and Restricted Investments.
lme_vectors.quote: any dividend or other distribution (whether in cash, securities or other property) with respect to any Equity Interest of any Loan Party ... and (ii) any Restricted Investment.

lme_vectors.rationale: The excerpt does not reproduce the voting/amendment or sacred-rights provisions needed to assess uptier/priming protection; Required Lenders is defined as >50% but no pro-rata sharing or lien-subordination sacred right is quoted.
lme_vectors.basis: Definition of 'Required Lenders' (more than 50% of Total Outstandings and Aggregate Commitments) is provided, but no amendment sacred-rights or priming carve-out clause appears in the text.
lme_vectors.quote: means, as of any date of determination, Lenders having more than 50% of the sum of the (a) Total Outstandings and (b) Aggregate Commitments

WHEREAS, the Borrowers requested
that the Lenders under this Agreement as of the Closing Date extend credit to
the Borrowers in an aggregate principal amount of $1,077,251,291
in the form of (i) Tranche A Term Loans (as herein defined) in an aggregate
principal amount of $128,865,980 and (ii) Tranche
B Term Loans (as herein defined) in an aggregate principal amount of
$948,385,311, in each case to be funded on the Closing Date subject
to the terms and conditions of this Agreement; and

WHEREAS, concurrently herewith, the Lead Borrower, as lender, Deutsche Bank AG
New York Branch, as the administrative agent for the lenders thereunder,
Trinseo Lead Borrower and Trinseo Co-Borrower (each as defined herein) will
enter into that certain 2023 Incremental and Refinancing Amendment to the
Trinseo Credit Agreement (as defined herein) (the “ 2023 Incremental and
Refinancing Amendment”), dated as of the date hereof, pursuant to which the
Lead Borrower will extend credit to the Trinseo Lead Borrower and the Trinseo
Co-Borrower in the form of (i) incremental term loans (which shall constitute
Incremental Term Loans (as defined in the Trinseo Credit Agreement) under
Section 2.16of the Trinseo Credit Agreement) in an original aggregate principal
amount equal to $268,041,238
(the “”) and (ii) refinancing term loans (which constitute Refinancing Term
Loans
(as defined in the Trinseo Credit Agreement) under of the Trinseo Credit
Agreement) in an original aggregate
principal amount equal to $680,344,073 (the “”).
